title: New Testament Foundations — Pauline Epistles
subtitle: Paul's Letters Across Five Letter Groups
description: A structured study of Paul's thirteen letters — from Romans through Philemon — examining gospel theology, church ethics, christology, and pastoral instruction across five letter groups.
learning_outcomes: Explain Paul's gospel theology as developed in Romans
Identify the major ethical issues Paul addresses in Corinthians
Describe Paul's argument for justification by faith in Galatians
Explain the Christ-hymn in Philippians 2 and its theological function
Identify the pastoral concerns of the Pastoral Epistles

theological_framework.emphasis: Gospel as the foundation of both doctrine and community life

units.title: Romans
units.description: Paul's fullest gospel exposition — righteousness by faith for Jew and Gentile (Lessons 1–4)

units.title: Corinthians
units.description: Gospel and community ethics in a divided, gifted, and troubled church (Lessons 5–10)

units.title: Galatians and Ephesians
units.description: Freedom from the law and identity in Christ (Lessons 11–14)

units.title: Prison and Thessalonian Epistles
units.description: Joy, supremacy of Christ, and hope in the last day (Lessons 15–21)

units.title: Pastoral Epistles and Philemon
units.description: Church order, sound teaching, and gospel reconciliation (Lessons 22–27)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

content_scope.subject: Pauline Epistles
content_scope.primary_texts: Romans, 1-2 Corinthians, Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians, 1-2 Thessalonians, 1-2 Timothy, Titus, Philemon

questions: What is Paul's thesis statement in Romans 1:16-17?
What does Paul say is the greatest spiritual gift in 1 Corinthians 13?
What is Paul's argument against those requiring circumcision in Galatians?
What does Paul call the armor of God in Ephesians 6?
What does Paul tell Timothy about the purpose of Scripture in 2 Timothy 3:16?

next_course.title: New Testament Foundations — General Epistles and Revelation
next_course.description: Study Hebrews through Revelation — the climax of NT instruction and canon
